description_long: This is a Corinthian-style ancient Greek helmet, crafted from metal with a prominent crest running from front to back. The helmet features a full-face guard with a T-shaped opening for the eyes and mouth, and cheek plates that extend downward for additional protection. The surface is metallic with visible scratches, dents, and patches of red discoloration, possibly representing blood or rust. Decorative circular elements are present on each side near the ears, and the crest is ribbed, adding both structural strength and visual distinction. The interior is hollow, designed to fit over a human head, and the overall form is robust and battle-worn.
description: A metallic Corinthian-style helmet with a prominent crest, full-face guard, and battle-worn surface featuring red stains and decorative side elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the T-shaped eye and mouth openings, cheek plates, and the central crest.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the ribbed crest, circular ear decoration, and red stains on the upper surface.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the helmet and the full length of the crest.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with visible crest, cheek plate, and circular ear decoration.
